chunks.text: ORDER SHEET HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P. No.D-6248 of 2021 ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Date Order with signature of Judge ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 1. For orders on Misc. No.26511/2021 (U/A) 2. For orders on Misc. No.26512/2021 (Ex/A). 3. For hearing of main case -------------------------------------------------------------- 04.11.2021. Rana Sakhawat Ali, advocate for the petitioners. >><< 1. Urgency granted. 2. Exemption is granted subject to all just exceptions. 3. Learned counsel states that the petitioners had previously filed a petition before Lahore High Court for redressal of their grievance, which was returned to them on the ground that since the respondent No.4, against whom relief has been sought is stationed in Karachi, hence this petition lies before this Court. Learned counsel states that the petitioners have moved an application dated 07.06.2021 ‘for grant of disparity reduction allowance’ to the respondent No.4, which even after passage of substantial time has not been decided. Learned counsel states that the petitioners would be satisfied and would not press this petition if directions are issued to the respondent No.4 to decide the said application filed by the petitioners within a shortest possible time. Mr. Muhammad Nishat Warsi, learned DAG present in Court in some other matter has waived notice and has given no objection for disposal of this petition on the aforesaid prayer made by the counsel for the petitioners. In view of the above, the instant petition is disposed of as not pressed, by directing the respondent No.4 to consider and decide the application filed by the petitioners in accordance with law, after affording the opportunity of hearing to them, within a period of one month from the date of the receipt of this order.

Muhammad Nishat Warsi, learned DAG present in Court in some other matter has waived notice and has given no objection for disposal of this petition on the aforesaid prayer made by the counsel for the petitioners. In view of the above, the instant petition is disposed of as not pressed, by directing the respondent No.4 to consider and decide the application filed by the petitioners in accordance with law, after affording the opportunity of hearing to them, within a period of one month from the date of the receipt of this order. Let a copy of this order be sent to the concerned respondent for information and compliance. JUDGE JUDGE Gulzar/PA
